A1.name: Macro / circuit generators
A1.artifact: Macro templates, cell choices, precision datapaths

A2.name: Simulators & cost models
A2.artifact: Component hierarchy, energy/latency LUTs, nonideality tables

A3.name: Mapping / scheduling / DSE
A3.artifact: Tensor tiles, loop nests, array bindings, replication choices

A4.name: Explicit IR / dialect / ISA stacks
A4.artifact: Graph IRs, MLIR dialects, hardware-resource IRs, meta-ops, ISA streams

A5.name: Narrow end-to-end co-design
A5.artifact: One architecture by one workload family

A6.name: Programming / runtime / benchmark
A6.artifact: Runtime APIs, DPU partitioning, host-device movement, benchmark harnesses

B1.name: Config-as-IR / search state
B1.desc: Workload, hardware, device, search, and PPA descriptions act as the operative middle layer.

B2.name: Graph-as-IR
B2.desc: The middle layer is an operator DAG or model graph annotated, partitioned, fused, duplicated, or scheduled.

B3.name: Loop / tensor schedule
B3.desc: Affine, tensor-expression, einsum, linalg, LoopTree, or polyhedral schedule abstractions drive placement and timing.

B4.name: Hardware-resource IR
B4.desc: The stack explicitly names CIM resources such as chips, cores, arrays, crossbars, wordlines, ADCs, buffers, or NoC links.

B5.name: Instruction / meta-op / ILA
B5.desc: A backend boundary is expressed as pseudo-instructions, meta-operators, microprograms, command traces, or virtual assembly.

B6.name: Accuracy / nonideality model
B6.desc: Analog error, quantization, variation, faults, drift, IR drop, or learned nonideality models are central.

B7.name: Runtime-state abstraction
B7.desc: The central abstraction is request state, KV cache, dynamic masks, batching, placement, or host-device execution state.

coverage_dimensions.label: Frontend
coverage_dimensions.desc: Imports or captures application/model programs, such as ONNX, TorchScript, C/C++, Verilog, kernels, or task graphs.

coverage_dimensions.label: Graph IR
coverage_dimensions.desc: Represents neural-network or task graphs as an explicit transform target.

coverage_dimensions.label: Loop / tensor schedule
coverage_dimensions.desc: Uses loop, tensor, affine, polyhedral, or dataflow schedules as explicit optimization objects.

coverage_dimensions.label: CIM resource model
coverage_dimensions.desc: Models CIM hardware hierarchy and resources: macro, array, crossbar, bank, core, PE, NoC, buffer, or peripheral.

coverage_dimensions.label: Mapping / DSE
coverage_dimensions.desc: Optimizes placement, tiling, replication, partitioning, routing, or hardware design choices.

coverage_dimensions.label: ISA / meta-op
coverage_dimensions.desc: Defines instructions, pseudo-instructions, meta-ops, command traces, or microprograms.

coverage_dimensions.label: Simulator / cost
coverage_dimensions.desc: Provides latency, energy, area, utilization, accuracy, or cycle/performance modeling.

coverage_dimensions.label: Accuracy / nonideality
coverage_dimensions.desc: Tracks quantization, analog nonidealities, faults, approximate arithmetic, or chip-calibrated errors.

coverage_dimensions.label: Runtime / state
coverage_dimensions.desc: Manages dynamic placement, batching, KV state, host-device transfer, DPU/tasklet state, or execution control.

coverage_dimensions.label: Macro / circuit generation
coverage_dimensions.desc: Generates or optimizes RTL, layouts, macros, circuit templates, cells, or physical-design artifacts.

coverage_dimensions.label: Real hardware / chip loop
coverage_dimensions.desc: Uses measured silicon, real UPMEM-class systems, chip-in-loop emulation, or silicon-calibrated validation.

coverage_dimensions.label: Artifact / benchmark
coverage_dimensions.desc: Provides a reusable artifact, benchmark suite, public tool, or reproducible workflow evidence.

rewrite_types.mode: mode selection across compute, memory, or device classes
rewrite_types.numeric: numeric format, precision, quantization, or approximation
rewrite_types.runtime: runtime scheduling, placement, batching, or data movement
rewrite_types.accuracy: fault, variation, calibration, or accuracy handling
